issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://lkfl2.nalog.ru/lkfl](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Flkfl2.nalog.ru%2Flkfl)

### Comment
> Добрый день!
> При использовании DNS 94.140.14.14, 94.140.15.15 не работает личный кабинет ФНС России,
> например, https://lkfl2.nalog.ru/lkfl.
> И, соответственно, их мобильное приложение.
> Проверьте, пожалуйста.
> Переключил на 14.140, 14.141 - тоже не работает.
> Пока пришлось вернуться на DNS провайдера.
> 
> Поддержка решить вопрос не смогла: 
> 
> Здравствуйте!
> 
> Со своей стороны блокировки не видим:
>  
> 
> 
> Сообщите об этой проблеме вот так, пожалуйста: https://reports.adguard.com/ru/new_issue.html. Наши разработчики фильтров ещё проверят у себя.
>  
> Сообщите, если есть другие вопросы или нужна помощь 🙏️

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://cero-t.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/03/09/080000](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcero-t.hatenadiary.jp%2Fentry%2F2026%2F03%2F09%2F080000) ### Comment > The “XX USER” display disappears. > I suspect the Social filter is the cause. > I think this issue is likely related to that other issue. https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/227471 > > > Also, Hatena Blog uses many domains, so this should be addressed for all of them. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/699a0d5k4zokcgkkwg0g44ooko0o4cwiyrr0hpfmao.jpg?nc=1)

AdGuard Mobile Ads filter

comment.body:
I think it doesn't work. I need:

```adb
cero-t.hatenadiary.jp#@#a[href*="//b.hatena.ne.jp/entry/"]:not(.http-bookmark)
```

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://duckduckgo.com/?q=test&ia=web&atb=v509-1](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fduckduckgo.com%2F%3Fq%3Dtest%26ia%3Dweb%26atb%3Dv509-1) ### Comment > DuckDuckGo search has suddenly stopped working with Adguard enabled. Trying to search from my browser bar returns the attached error message. If I manually type in duckduckgo.com I can go to the DuckDuckGo home page, but as soon as I try to search anything on their website I just get the same error message again. Disabling my other extensions (including DuckDuckGo Privacy Essentials) has no effect on removing the error message. The only thing that fixes it is disabling Adguard. The error message also seems to only apply to DuckDuckGo. When I test Google, it loads my searches just fine there. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/4v5a2kwwqdyckg00sk4go440wwkw4swq1rhderiihc.png?nc=1)
